us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Trump says he will ask Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
bullets: Trump said he plans to seek a rehearing after the Supreme Court rejected his birthright citizenship restrictions.
The court’s June ruling struck down his executive order by a 6-3 vote.
The new request is a long-shot effort to reverse a major immigration defeat.

articles.title: Justice Department warns states over noncitizen voting rules
bullets: The DOJ sent letters to election officials in all 50 states and Washington, D.C.
Officials were warned they could face prosecution if they knowingly allow noncitizens to vote or stay on rolls.
Voting-rights advocates said documented noncitizen voting remains extremely rare.

articles.title: Family seeks independent probe after ICE officer fatally shoots man in Houston
bullets: A Mexican national was shot and killed during an ICE operation in Houston.
His family said he had no criminal convictions and was driving workers to a job site.
Mexico’s president said her government is preparing legal measures over the killing.

articles.title: US jobless claims dip to 215,000
bullets: Initial unemployment claims fell by 2,000 in the week ended July 4.
The Labor Department reported 215,000 new claims on Thursday.
Weekly claims remain near levels consistent with historically low layoffs.

articles.title: Haley Stevens pitches electability in pivotal Michigan Senate primary
bullets: Rep. Haley Stevens is emphasizing her record winning competitive races.
Michigan’s Aug. 4 Democratic Senate primary is now a major battleground contest.
Democrats view the seat as critical to any path back to a Senate majority.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: US and Iran exchange fresh fire across the Gulf
bullets: The U.S. launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday.
Iran responded by targeting U.S.-allied Gulf states including Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar.
The escalation threatened an interim deal meant to wind down the war.

articles.title: UK Labour opens nominations to replace Keir Starmer
bullets: Labour opened nominations Thursday for a leadership contest to replace Starmer as prime minister.
Andy Burnham is expected to be the only major candidate.
Starmer said last month he would resign once Labour selected a successor.

articles.title: Venezuela quake survivors face worsening water and sanitation shortages
bullets: Thousands affected by June’s earthquakes are struggling to access clean water.
Residents in La Guaira are improvising showers and toilets as infrastructure remains damaged.
The disaster has left many communities dependent on makeshift relief arrangements.

articles.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russian oil facilities
bullets: Ukraine said its drones struck oil infrastructure across several Russian regions.
A fire was reported at an oil depot in Tver after a drone strike.
Zelenskyy said targets also included fuel infrastructure in Ufa and an oil-loading terminal in Rostov.

articles.title: Oil rises as renewed US-Iran fighting shakes markets
bullets: Oil prices rose Thursday after the U.S. launched fresh strikes against Iran.
Reuters reported the escalation hurt hopes for a full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
The strait is a key route for roughly one-fifth of pre-war global oil supplies.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Australia agrees to sell uranium to India after Modi-Albanese talks
bullets: Australia and India signed an administrative deal enabling uranium exports for peaceful purposes.
Modi and Albanese announced the agreement after meeting in Melbourne.
The leaders also pledged deeper defense and Indo-Pacific security cooperation.

articles.title: India urges restraint as West Asia tensions escalate
bullets: India called on parties in the conflict to return to dialogue and diplomacy.
The MEA statement followed renewed U.S.-Iran strikes and threats of further escalation.
Delhi said a peaceful and lasting solution should be pursued through talks.

articles.title: Assault on doctor triggers protests across Maharashtra
bullets: Resident doctors in government hospitals planned black-ribbon protests on Thursday.
The action followed an alleged assault on medical staff at a hospital in Dombivli.
Nurses’ groups warned of further protest if stronger action is not taken.

articles.title: Indian agencies shared gangster hideout details with US officials
bullets: Indian agencies reportedly shared lists of Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar associates with U.S. officials.
The coordination followed recent meetings involving central agencies and law enforcement partners.
Officials said evidence and possible hideouts were verified as part of Operation ‘Hardball.’

articles.title: Baruipur rape-murder accused killed in Bengal police ‘encounter’
bullets: Police said Prabhash Mondol was shot during a crime-scene reconstruction late Tuesday.
He was a main accused in the gangrape and murder of an 11-year-old girl in Baruipur.
The case has triggered protests, arrests and a Special Investigation Team probe.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top US market movers from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Broadcom rises after Apple expands US chip deal
bullets: Apple plans to spend more than $30 billion under a multiyear Broadcom chip agreement.
Broadcom shares rose more than 4% after details of the deal were reported.
The deal runs through 2031 and includes expansion at Broadcom’s Fort Collins, Colorado facility.

articles.title: Dollar Tree gains after Goldman Sachs and Raymond James upgrades
bullets: Dollar Tree shares rose after analyst upgrades from Goldman Sachs and Raymond James.
Raymond James moved the stock to Outperform with a $140 target.
Goldman Sachs upgraded the shares to Neutral and raised its price target to $125.

articles.title: DraftKings and Flutter move after Michael Burry discloses stakes
bullets: Michael Burry said he bought shares of Flutter and DraftKings.
He is betting regulatory scrutiny will reduce threats from prediction markets.
Reuters reported the position is weighted roughly 60/40 toward Flutter.

articles.title: Bloom Energy drops after Hunterbrook short report
bullets: Hunterbrook Media published a critical report on Bloom Energy’s supply chain and capacity assumptions.
The report disclosed Hunterbrook Capital is short Bloom Energy.
Chinese market coverage reported Bloom shares fell sharply after the report.

articles.title: US stocks steady while crude rises on Iran escalation
bullets: U.S. markets were mixed as renewed Iran-U.S. attacks kept investors cautious.
Oil prices rose modestly after new Middle East strikes.
The AP reported the moves came after sharp swings in the prior session.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Top corporate and economy stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in AI-driven restructuring
bullets: Microsoft is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs.
The company is restructuring parts of its commercial and Xbox businesses.
Reuters reported the cuts come as Big Tech shifts spending toward AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Starbucks develops AI tools to reduce reliance on Microsoft and IBM software
bullets: Starbucks is building in-house AI-assisted tools that could replace some outside software.
Bloomberg reported the tools target inventory and maintenance systems.
The company reportedly spends about $400 million a year on software.

articles.title: Egg producers settle price-fixing claims with DOJ and states
bullets: Three major egg producers agreed to settle alleged price-fixing claims.
The settlement includes $3.3 million in payments and 53 million donated eggs.
The DOJ and 17 states accused the companies of colluding to inflate egg-price benchmarks.

articles.title: Apple commits more than $30 billion to Broadcom chip supply
bullets: Apple expanded a chip-supply agreement with Broadcom through 2031.
The deal covers U.S.-made radio-frequency chips used in Apple devices.
Broadcom plans to invest $1.5 billion to expand its Colorado factory.

articles.title: Samsung flags 19-fold profit jump as AI memory boom continues
bullets: Samsung Electronics forecast a 19-fold jump in second-quarter operating profit.
AI data-center demand pushed memory-chip prices to record highs.
Shares still fell as investors questioned how long the AI chip boom can last.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Meta reportedly plans Iris AI chip production and bigger compute buildout
bullets: Reuters reported Meta plans to put its in-house Iris AI chip into production in September.
The company is also looking to double computing capacity to 14GW in 2027.
The move reflects Meta’s effort to reduce dependence on outside AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Apple and Broadcom expand US-made wireless chip production
bullets: Apple announced a multiyear Broadcom deal worth more than $30 billion.
The companies plan to produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made custom chips.
The components will support wireless connectivity in Apple products.

articles.title: Zuckerberg says Meta’s AI agent development is slower than expected
bullets: Zuckerberg told staff that AI agent development had not accelerated as expected, Reuters reported.
The comments came during an internal town hall reviewed by Reuters.
Meta still expects benefits from AI investments over the next several months.

articles.title: Samsung’s AI memory surge boosts profits but raises boom-cycle concerns
bullets: Samsung’s preliminary results showed a sharp profit rebound driven by AI memory demand.
Demand for DRAM, NAND and AI server components has lifted chip prices.
The stock fell as investors weighed whether the AI infrastructure boom can sustain pricing.

articles.title: AI hiring in India rises even as overall IT recruitment falls
bullets: AI hiring in India’s IT sector rose 16% year over year in June.
Overall IT jobs fell 3%, according to Naukri’s JobSpeak report cited by Reuters.
The trend points to companies shifting hiring toward specialized AI roles.
